user_query:
I need a full product differentiation analysis for my insulated travel mug based on provided competitor data and reviews. Please do the following:

1) Read input/product_data.json. It contains:
- your_product: asin, name, price, rating, review_count, features
- competitors: list with name, price, rating, review_count
- competitor_negative_reviews: array of strings
- my_positive_reviews: array of strings

2) Perform a Level L4 analysis and produce two outputs:
- output/summary.json (machine-checkable summary)
- output/report.md (human-readable strategy report)

3) Exact requirements for output/summary.json:
- analysis_level: string, one of L1/L2/L3/L4
- comparison: object with
  - products: array of product names in order [mine, comp1, comp2, ...]
  - prices: array of numbers in the same order
  - ratings: array of numbers in the same order
  - review_counts: array of integers in the same order
  - avg_price: number (mean of all prices, including mine), keep full precision or to 2 decimals is fine
  - avg_rating: number (mean of all ratings, including mine)
  - price_position: "above_avg" or "below_avg" for my product versus the avg_price
  - rating_position: "above_avg" or "below_avg" for my product versus the avg_rating
- pain_points: array of objects with exactly these fields for each detected category
  - category: one of ["quality","function","design","size","shipping","value"] (lowercase)
  - frequency: integer
  - severity: one of ["high","medium","low"] using this rule: high if frequency >= 5, medium if 2-4, low if 1
- selling_points: array of objects with exactly these fields for each detected category
  - category: one of ["quality","function","design","value","service"] (lowercase)
  - frequency: integer
  - strength: one of ["strong","medium","weak"] using this rule: strong if frequency >= 5, medium if 2-4, weak if 1
- opportunities: array of objects; include at least three items with an "angle" field whose value is one of ["function","quality","design","price","service","audience","scenario","brand"] (lowercase). Ensure at least one with angle="price", one with angle="quality", and one with angle="audience".
- positioning: object with
  - position_type: one of ["premium","value","balanced","niche","innovation"]

4) Extraction rules (must follow for determinism):
- Pain point mining from competitor_negative_reviews uses keyword matching by category. Use these English keyword sets:
  - quality: ["cheap","broke","broken","flimsy","poor quality","fell apart","defective","doesn't last","stopped working"]
  - function: ["doesn't work","not working","malfunction","missing feature","can't","won't","failed","useless"]
  - design: ["ugly","looks cheap","bulky","heavy","uncomfortable","awkward","hard to use","confusing"]
  - size: ["too small","too big","wrong size","doesn't fit","smaller than expected","bigger than"]
  - shipping: ["late","damaged","wrong item","missing parts","packaging"]
  - value: ["overpriced","not worth","waste of money","rip off","too expensive"]
- Selling point extraction from my_positive_reviews uses keyword matching by category:
  - quality: ["solid","sturdy","durable","well made","high quality","premium","excellent","perfect"]
  - function: ["works great","works perfectly","easy to use","convenient","efficient","powerful","fast"]
  - design: ["beautiful","sleek","stylish","modern","compact","lightweight","elegant"]
  - value: ["great value","worth it","good price","affordable","best purchase","recommend"]
  - service: ["great service","fast shipping","well packaged","responsive seller"]
- Counting rule: For each review string, a category counts at most once even if multiple keywords from the same category appear in that review; different categories in the same review each count once. Tally frequency by category accordingly.

5) Report requirements for output/report.md (human-readable):
- Include these clearly labeled sections as headings (case-insensitive acceptable):
  - "Competitor Comparison Matrix" (show side-by-side prices, ratings, and counts, and state price_position and rating_position)
  - "Top Pain Points" (list categories with frequency and severity, plus 1 short example per category if available)
  - "Your Unique Selling Points" (list categories with frequency and strength)
  - "Differentiation Opportunities"
  - "Market Positioning Strategy" (state position_type and 2-3 marketing angles)
  - "Action Plan" (at least 3 prioritized actions)

6) Deliverables:
- Write the machine-checkable summary to output/summary.json following the schema above.
- Write the human-readable strategy report to output/report.md.
- Use relative paths only (no absolute paths).
